item.title: BOOTLOOP SAVER [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:title: BOOTLOOP SAVER [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:author: revwhiteshadow
item.itunes:summary:
BOOTLOOP SAVER
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
BOOTLOOP SAVER by revWhiteShadow is a revolutionary Magisk module designed to automatically disable any problematic modules causing bootloop issues on your Android device. Developed by the talented developer revWhiteShadow, this module acts as a safeguard, ensuring your device boots smoothly without being hindered by conflicting or problematic modules. It's the go-to solution for Magisk users who want a hassle-free, error-free booting experience. With BOOTLOOP SAVER, you can enjoy the benefits of Magisk modules while maintaining the stability and reliability of your Android device.
[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.
Select the module from storage.
Install the module.
Reboot the virtual device within LDPlayer.
[read more]

item.content:encoded:
BOOTLOOP SAVER
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
BOOTLOOP SAVER by revWhiteShadow is a revolutionary Magisk module designed to automatically disable any problematic modules causing bootloop issues on your Android device. Developed by the talented developer revWhiteShadow, this module acts as a safeguard, ensuring your device boots smoothly without being hindered by conflicting or problematic modules. It's the go-to solution for Magisk users who want a hassle-free, error-free booting experience. With BOOTLOOP SAVER, you can enjoy the benefits of Magisk modules while maintaining the stability and reliability of your Android device.
[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.
Select the module from storage.
Install the module.
Reboot the virtual device within LDPlayer.
[read more]

item.title: GS GRAPHIC RENDERINGS [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:title: GS GRAPHIC RENDERINGS [System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS)]
item.itunes:author: revwhiteshadow
item.itunes:summary:
GS GRAPHIC RENDERINGS
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
GS Graphic Renderings Tweak is a powerful tool designed to enhance the graphic rendering capabilities of Android devices. With this tweak, users have the freedom to choose between three advanced rendering options: OpenGL ES, Sikagl, and VulkanSika.
OpenGL ES, a widely-used graphics API, provides a solid foundation for rendering graphics on Android devices. It offers a high level of compatibility and performance, ensuring smooth and visually appealing graphics for gaming and multimedia applications.

Sikagl, an innovative rendering framework, introduces cutting-edge features and optimizations to deliver stunning visuals. With Sikagl, users can experience enhanced shading, lighting, and special effects, pushing the boundaries of graphic rendering on their Android devices.

VulkanSika, a modern and efficient graphics API, takes advantage of the latest hardware capabilities to deliver unparalleled performance and visual fidelity. By leveraging multi-threading and low-level hardware control, VulkanSika optimizes resource utilization and provides a smooth and responsive user experience, especially for graphically-intensive applications.

With the GS Graphic Renderings Tweak, users can seamlessly switch between these rendering options based on their preferences and specific application requirements. Whether they seek compatibility, advanced features, or maximum performance, this tweak empowers users to unlock the full potential of their Android devices and enjoy a customized and immersive graphical experience.

[h2]Linux Installation[/h2]

Unzip the ZIP File:
unzip
Navigate to the Extracted Folder:

cd extracted_folder
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
./
For 64-bit systems:
./

[h2]Windows Installation[/h2]
Extract the zip:
Use a tool like WinRAR, 7-Zip, or the built-in Windows tool to extract the contents of the APK file.
Open Command Prompt:

Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
bash
For 64-bit systems:
bash

[h2]Android Installation / BlissOS / Other Emulator Installation[/h2]
Open Magisk / Kernel SU:

Ensure you have Magisk or Kernel SU installed on your device or emulator.
Go to the Module Section:

Open the Magisk app and navigate to the Modules section.
Select the Module from Storage:

Tap on "Select from Storage" and choose the previously downloaded file.
Install the Module:

After selecting the file, press "OK". The module will install itself.
Reboot the Device:

Once the installation is complete, reboot your device to finalize the installation.

[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.
Select the module from storage.
Install the module.
Reboot the virtual device within LDPlayer.
[read more]
item.description:
GS GRAPHIC RENDERINGS
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
GS Graphic Renderings Tweak is a powerful tool designed to enhance the graphic rendering capabilities of Android devices. With this tweak, users have the freedom to choose between three advanced rendering options: OpenGL ES, Sikagl, and VulkanSika.
OpenGL ES, a widely-used graphics API, provides a solid foundation for rendering graphics on Android devices. It offers a high level of compatibility and performance, ensuring smooth and visually appealing graphics for gaming and multimedia applications.

Sikagl, an innovative rendering framework, introduces cutting-edge features and optimizations to deliver stunning visuals. With Sikagl, users can experience enhanced shading, lighting, and special effects, pushing the boundaries of graphic rendering on their Android devices.

VulkanSika, a modern and efficient graphics API, takes advantage of the latest hardware capabilities to deliver unparalleled performance and visual fidelity. By leveraging multi-threading and low-level hardware control, VulkanSika optimizes resource utilization and provides a smooth and responsive user experience, especially for graphically-intensive applications.

With the GS Graphic Renderings Tweak, users can seamlessly switch between these rendering options based on their preferences and specific application requirements. Whether they seek compatibility, advanced features, or maximum performance, this tweak empowers users to unlock the full potential of their Android devices and enjoy a customized and immersive graphical experience.

[h2]Linux Installation[/h2]

Unzip the ZIP File:
unzip
Navigate to the Extracted Folder:

cd extracted_folder
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
./
For 64-bit systems:
./

[h2]Windows Installation[/h2]
Extract the zip:
Use a tool like WinRAR, 7-Zip, or the built-in Windows tool to extract the contents of the APK file.
Open Command Prompt:

Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
bash
For 64-bit systems:
bash

[h2]Android Installation / BlissOS / Other Emulator Installation[/h2]
Open Magisk / Kernel SU:

Ensure you have Magisk or Kernel SU installed on your device or emulator.
Go to the Module Section:

Open the Magisk app and navigate to the Modules section.
Select the Module from Storage:

Tap on "Select from Storage" and choose the previously downloaded file.
Install the Module:

After selecting the file, press "OK". The module will install itself.
Reboot the Device:

Once the installation is complete, reboot your device to finalize the installation.

[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.

item.content:encoded:
GS GRAPHIC RENDERINGS
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
GS Graphic Renderings Tweak is a powerful tool designed to enhance the graphic rendering capabilities of Android devices. With this tweak, users have the freedom to choose between three advanced rendering options: OpenGL ES, Sikagl, and VulkanSika.
OpenGL ES, a widely-used graphics API, provides a solid foundation for rendering graphics on Android devices. It offers a high level of compatibility and performance, ensuring smooth and visually appealing graphics for gaming and multimedia applications.

Sikagl, an innovative rendering framework, introduces cutting-edge features and optimizations to deliver stunning visuals. With Sikagl, users can experience enhanced shading, lighting, and special effects, pushing the boundaries of graphic rendering on their Android devices.

VulkanSika, a modern and efficient graphics API, takes advantage of the latest hardware capabilities to deliver unparalleled performance and visual fidelity. By leveraging multi-threading and low-level hardware control, VulkanSika optimizes resource utilization and provides a smooth and responsive user experience, especially for graphically-intensive applications.

With the GS Graphic Renderings Tweak, users can seamlessly switch between these rendering options based on their preferences and specific application requirements. Whether they seek compatibility, advanced features, or maximum performance, this tweak empowers users to unlock the full potential of their Android devices and enjoy a customized and immersive graphical experience.

[h2]Linux Installation[/h2]

Unzip the ZIP File:
unzip
Navigate to the Extracted Folder:

cd extracted_folder
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
./
For 64-bit systems:
./

Extract the zip:
Use a tool like WinRAR, 7-Zip, or the built-in Windows tool to extract the contents of the APK file.

Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.

Open the Magisk app and navigate to the Modules section.
Select the Module from Storage:

Tap on "Select from Storage" and choose the previously downloaded file.

After selecting the file, press "OK". The module will install itself.
Reboot the Device:

Once the installation is complete, reboot your device to finalize the installation.

Install the Module:

After selecting the file, press "OK". The module will install itself.
Reboot the Device:

Once the installation is complete, reboot your device to finalize the installation.
[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.
Select the module from storage.
Install the module.
Reboot the virtual device within LDPlayer.
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
PHOENIX SDM 8
(System Tools (NOT OS or ROMS))
[url=https://www.pling.com/p/2050006/]MTK Optimization [/url]
[url=https://www.pling.com/p/2050005/]SDM 8 Optimization [/url]
[url=https://www.pling.com/p/2050004/]SDM 7 Optimization [/url]
[url=https://www.pling.com/p/2019745/]SDM 6 Optimization [/url]
[h2]Linux Installation[/h2]

Unzip the ZIP File:
unzip
Navigate to the Extracted Folder:

cd extracted_folder
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
./
For 64-bit systems:
./

[h2]Windows Installation[/h2]
Extract the zip:
Use a tool like WinRAR, 7-Zip, or the built-in Windows tool to extract the contents of the APK file.
Open Command Prompt:

Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.
Run the Installer:

For 32-bit systems:
bash
For 64-bit systems:
bash

[h2]Android Installation / BlissOS / Other Emulator Installation[/h2]
Open Magisk / Kernel SU:

Ensure you have Magisk or Kernel SU installed on your device or emulator.
Go to the Module Section:

Open the Magisk app and navigate to the Modules section.
Select the Module from Storage:

Tap on "Select from Storage" and choose the previously downloaded file.
Install the Module:

After selecting the file, press "OK". The module will install itself.
Reboot the Device:

Once the installation is complete, reboot your device to finalize the installation.
[h2]Virtualisation Installation[/h2]
Step 1: Open LDPlayer,android studio or other simulation
Ensure LDPlayer, an Android emulator, is installed on your computer. Open LDPlayer to begin the process.

Step 2: Follow Android Installation
Within LDPlayer, follow the steps outlined in the Android Installation section:

Open Magisk or KernelSU within the emulator.
Navigate to the Modules section.
Select the module from storage.
Install the module.
Reboot the virtual device within LDPlayer.
[read more]

item.title: AAAAXY [Jump N Run]
item.itunes:title: AAAAXY [Jump N Run]
item.itunes:author: divverent
item.itunes:summary:
AAAAXY
(Jump N Run)
AAAAXY is a nonlinear 2D puzzle platformer taking place in impossible spaces.
Although your general goal is reaching the surprising end of the game, you are encouraged to set your own goals while playing. Exploration will be rewarded, and secrets await you!

So jump and run around, and enjoy losing your sense of orientation in this World of Wicked Weirdness. Find out what Van Vlijmen will make you do. Pick a path, get inside a Klein Bottle, recognize some memes, and by all means: don't look up.

And beware of a minor amount of trolling.

To reach the end, a new player will take about 4 to 6 hours, a full playthrough can be finished in about 1 hour and the end can be reached in about 15 minutes.
[read more]
item.description:
AAAAXY
(Jump N Run)
AAAAXY is a nonlinear 2D puzzle platformer taking place in impossible spaces.
Although your general goal is reaching the surprising end of the game, you are encouraged to set your own goals while playing. Exploration will be rewarded, and secrets await you!

So jump and run around, and enjoy losing your sense of orientation in this World of Wicked Weirdness. Find out what Van Vlijmen will make you do. Pick a path, get inside a Klein Bottle, recognize some memes, and by all means: don't look up.

And beware of a minor amount of trolling.

To reach the end, a new player will take about 4 to 6 hours, a full playthrough can be finished in about 1 hour and the end can be reached in about 15 minutes.
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
AAAAXY
(Jump N Run)
AAAAXY is a nonlinear 2D puzzle platformer taking place in impossible spaces.
Although your general goal is reaching the surprising end of the game, you are encouraged to set your own goals while playing. Exploration will be rewarded, and secrets await you!

So jump and run around, and enjoy losing your sense of orientation in this World of Wicked Weirdness. Find out what Van Vlijmen will make you do. Pick a path, get inside a Klein Bottle, recognize some memes, and by all means: don't look up.

And beware of a minor amount of trolling.

To reach the end, a new player will take about 4 to 6 hours, a full playthrough can be finished in about 1 hour and the end can be reached in about 15 minutes.
[read more]
